name: CKAD Knowledge Graph
description: Knowledge graph for CKAD exam preparation. Nodes: categories -> exercises -> leaf tasks. Leaf tasks are simple tasks that can be completed with a single command. Each leaf task references a deduplicated flashcard in flashcards.json via flashcardId.

exercises.title: Create a namespace called 'mynamespace' and a pod with image nginx called nginx on this namespace

tasks.task: Create a namespace called mynamespace
Create a pod named nginx with image nginx in namespace mynamespace

exercises.title: Create the pod that was just described using YAML

tasks.task: Generate pod YAML without creating the pod (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --restart=Never --dry-run=client -n mynamespace -o yaml > pod.yaml

tasks.task: Create a resource from a YAML manifest file

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod (using kubectl command) that runs the command 'env'. Run it and see the output

tasks.task: Create a busybox pod that runs the command env
See the pod output in its logs

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod (using YAML) that runs the command 'env'. Run it and see the output

tasks.task: Generate YAML for a pod that runs a command, without creating it
tasks.command: kubectl run busybox --image=busybox --restart=Never --dry-run=client -o yaml --command -- env > envpod.yaml

tasks.task: Apply the manifest and check the logs

exercises.title: Get the YAML for a new namespace called 'myns' without creating it

tasks.task: Generate namespace YAML without creating it

exercises.title: Create the YAML for a new ResourceQuota called 'myrq' with hard limits of 1 CPU, 1G memory and 2 pods without creating it

tasks.task: Generate ResourceQuota YAML with hard limits without creating it

exercises.title: Get pods on all namespaces

tasks.task: List pods in all namespaces

exercises.title: Create a pod with image nginx called nginx and expose traffic on port 80

tasks.task: Create a pod declaring container port 80

exercises.title: Change pod's image to nginx:1.24.0. Observe that the container will be restarted as soon as the image gets pulled

tasks.task: Change the image of a running pod
Describe the pod to see events (container killed and recreated)
Watch the pod status changes
Check the pod's image using jsonpath

exercises.title: Get nginx pod's ip created in previous step, use a temp busybox image to wget its '/'

tasks.task: Get pod IPs (wide output)
Extract the pod IP with jsonpath
Run a temporary busybox pod and wget the nginx pod IP

exercises.title: Get pod's YAML

tasks.task: Get the YAML of a running pod

exercises.title: Get information about the pod, including details about potential issues (e.g. pod hasn't started)

tasks.task: Describe the pod (events, potential issues)

exercises.title: Get pod logs

tasks.task: Get pod logs

exercises.title: If pod crashed and restarted, get logs about the previous instance

tasks.task: Get logs of the previous container instance

exercises.title: Execute a simple shell on the nginx pod

tasks.task: Open an interactive shell in a running pod

exercises.title: Create a busybox pod that echoes 'hello world' and then exits

tasks.task: Run a one-shot pod with arguments and see its output

exercises.title: Do the same, but have the pod deleted automatically when it's completed

tasks.task: Run a temporary pod that is deleted automatically after it exits

exercises.title: Create an nginx pod and set an env value as 'var1=val1'. Check the env value existence within the pod

tasks.task: Create a pod with an environment variable
Print environment variables inside a running pod

exercises.title: Create a Pod with two containers, both with image busybox and command 'echo hello; sleep 3600'. Connect to the second container and run 'ls'

tasks.task: Generate a single-container pod YAML with the command as a base (dry-run)
tasks.command: kubectl run busybox --image=busybox --restart=Never -o yaml --dry-run=client -- /bin/sh -c 'echo hello;sleep 3600' > pod.yaml

tasks.task: Edit the YAML to add a second busybox container (different name) to the containers list
Create the pod from the manifest
Run a command in a specific container of a multi-container pod

exercises.title: Create a pod with an nginx container exposed on port 80. Add a busybox init container which writes 'Test' to /work-dir/index.html. Share an emptyDir volume mounted at /usr/share/nginx/html (nginx) and /work-dir (init container). Get the pod IP and wget it from a busybox pod

tasks.task: Generate a base pod YAML with container port 80 (dry-run)
Edit the YAML to add an emptyDir volume and mount it in the nginx container at /usr/share/nginx/html
Edit the YAML to add a busybox init container that writes the file and mounts the volume at /work-dir
Apply the pod manifest
Get the pod IP (wide output)
Run a temporary busybox pod and wget the pod IP
tasks.command: kubectl run box-test --image=busybox --restart=Never -it --rm -- /bin/sh -c "wget -O- $(kubectl get pod box -o jsonpath='{.status.podIP}')"
